top_signals.title: Slashing the Gas Tax Will Save Drivers 18 Cents Per Gallon, Agen Says
top_signals.summary: Jarrod Agen, Executive Director of the National Energy Dominance Council, talks about how the White House is trying to drive down gasoline prices. He says the "war on oil and gas" is over. Agen says slashing the federal gas tax would save drivers 18 cents per gallon. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Codelco Copper Overstatement Implies Output Sank to 27-Year Low
top_signals.summary: Codelco’s overstatement of copper production in December implies that last year’s annual output fell to the lowest level since 1998, eroding the Chilean company’s claims of a mining rebound.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Trump Advisor Predicts Gas Price Drop
top_signals.summary: Jarrod Agen, Executive Director of National Energy Dominance Council and President Trump's energy advisor joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how gas prices will drop fast once the Strait of Hormuz reopens. He also defends the administration’s aggressive “Drill Baby Drill” agenda. Agen touts record oil lease sales, LNG expansion, Alaska drilling, and a possible gas tax holiday as America races for “energy dominance.” (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Deere Shares Tumble With Farmers Facing ‘Ongoing Challenges’
top_signals.summary: Deere & Co. shares fell sharply as farm machinery sales stayed sluggish, raising questions on when the agriculture economy will start getting better.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
